run_mode_note: Stress test for productive-misfit case + weaker analyst. Case (Stripe 2011) is a 'when NOT to apply Spears' situation; framework prescribes outbound but right answer is to decline. Analyst is forced to Haiku via Agent tool model parameter. The hypothesis: does Haiku correctly decline outbound (showing framework understanding) or mechanically prescribe (failing the productive-misfit test)?
stress_test_summary: Haiku scored 1.000 on Predictable Revenue + Stripe 2011 — same as the default-model run. Haiku correctly identified the productive misfit and declined outbound ("DEFER Cold Calling 2.0") with five rationale points tied to framework conditions: pre-specialization, Seeds/Nets still scaling, ICP too loose for cold calling precision, no conversion math instrumentation, economics unproven. This is meaningful evidence the framework understanding generalizes across analyst strength, at least for this case shape. Combined with the Crossing the Chasm + Cursor 2026 stress test (Haiku 93.3%), the cross-book picture: Haiku is somewhat weaker on canonical-fit cases (one rubric item partial credit) but matches Sonnet on productive-misfit cases (full credit). The discrimination signal is in the rubric details (which failure modes are correctly cited), not the chasm-decline-or-prescribe gross call.
comparative_observation: Haiku's analysis was structurally heavier (more bullet formatting, more quantification like '95% Seeds, ~5% Nets, 0% Spears', explicit 'What Must Be True First' enumeration). Default subagent's analysis was structurally lighter prose. Both reached the same conclusion via different reasoning paths.

item_scores.reasoning: Names current de facto ICP at firmographic (early-stage, seed-Series-A, software/SaaS, consumer products, dev tools), behavioral (technical founder/lead engineer with code authority, P&L authority), trigger (building/scaling online transaction, seeking rapid integration). Tightness assessment explicit: 'Loose. Developers building online businesses describes hundreds of thousands.' Names what would tighten: cohort analysis on sign-up source, named expansion accounts (Lyft, Reddit, GitHub) signal.

All GTM roles confirmed absent (no SDR, AE, AM). Founder doing all three named explicitly with cited evidence (Patrick on HN, founders on tickets). Assessment frames it as appropriate-for-stage: 'Founder doing all three is a signature early-stage pattern when product fit is strong enough.'

All three sources named with quantitative breakdown: '95% Seeds, ~5% Nets, 0% Spears.' Each described with specifics (YC-network for Seeds, blog/HN for Nets, zero outbound for Spears). Conclusion: 'This is correct for the current growth stage and product-market fit quality. Nets are beginning to scale. Spears are not needed yet.'

DEFER outbound with 5 rationale points all tied to framework conditions: (1) pre-specialization (no SDR/AE structure), (2) Seeds and Nets still scaling at low CAC with high-velocity integration, (3) ICP too loose for cold calling precision, (4) no conversion math instrumentation, (5) economics unproven (SDR cost vs. payback math). Names what must be true first: SDR + AE hires, cohort analysis, tightened ICP, baseline conversion math, data on highest-value cohorts by lead source. Also names test approach when conditions are met: 'test Cold Calling 2.0 on named expansion accounts (Lyft/Reddit-tier customers who are not yet Stripe).'

Lists 6 specific ratios appropriate for current PLG/transactional motion: signup-to-paying, time-to-first-transaction, cohort retention by lead source, CAC by lead source, ARR per cohort, expansion rate. Frames as 'instrument these before SDR hire' — diagnostic infrastructure that needs to exist.

4 modes flagged with cited input evidence: founder doing all three roles (Patrick on HN, founders on tickets), no defined ICP / spray-and-pray risk if premature (cited verbatim input quote about no clear ICP), lead-source blindness (cited absent cohort analysis), potential overestimation of outbound fit (cited Braintree competitive pressure as forward-watch signal).

4 sequenced quarters (Q4 2011 → Q1 2012 → Q2 2012 → Q3 2012+) with explicit conditional gates (e.g., 'If Nets plateau, move to outbound planning. If Nets still scaling, defer outbound.'). Dependencies section lists explicit ordering rationale: 'Q4 data on lead sources and customer cohorts must precede Q1 ICP hypothesis. Hypothesis must precede SDR hire. SDR hire must precede outbound test. Outbound test results must precede AE hire.'

Two integer ratings: 'ICP Tightness: 3/10' (interpreted as confidence-in-the-current-ICP-being-tight; substantively communicates high confidence that the ICP is loose); 'Outbound Recommendation (DEFER): 8/10' with rationale. 3 open questions, all with named metrics: cohort retention by lead source (with 80% vs 40% threshold predictions), churn to competitor specifically Braintree, customer CAC and payback period (with $5K ARR vs $2K ARR economic threshold scenarios).
